descriptions.prod: The producer(s) writing 100-byte records, swept toward the cluster's write ceiling. (client.web speaks HTTP in the catalog; here it stands for the Kafka producer — the numeric capacity path is protocol-agnostic.)
descriptions.kafka: One Kafka cluster modeled as a single SDA node whose aggregate write capacity is the `throughput` cell (msg/s); nodeQueues reads it for rho, so the calibrated tunable is stream.kafka:throughput — SDA's ESTIMATED 100,000 msg/s default, corrected here to Kreps' measured 3-broker / 6-partition write throughput. Ordering and throughput are PER-PARTITION; SDA models the aggregate.
